root_text.sn13.9:1.2: “Seyyathāpi, bhikkhave, puriso himavato pabbatarājassa satta sāsapamattiyo pāsāṇasakkharā upanikkhipeyya.
root_text.sn13.9:1.4: katamaṁ nu kho bahutaraṁ, yā vā satta sāsapamattiyo pāsāṇasakkharā upanikkhittā yo vā himavā pabbatarājā”ti?
root_text.sn13.9:2.3: Neva satimaṁ kalaṁ upenti na sahassimaṁ kalaṁ upenti na satasahassimaṁ kalaṁ upenti himavantaṁ pabbatarājānaṁ upanidhāya satta sāsapamattiyo pāsāṇasakkharā upanikkhittā”ti.

translation_text.sn13.9:1.2: “Mendicants, suppose a person was to place seven pebbles the size of mustard seeds on the Himalayas, the king of mountains.
translation_text.sn13.9:1.4: Which is more: the seven pebbles the size of mustard seeds, or the Himalayas, the king of mountains?”
translation_text.sn13.9:2.1: “Sir, the Himalayas, the king of mountains, is certainly more.
translation_text.sn13.9:2.2: The seven pebbles the size of mustard seeds are tiny.
translation_text.sn13.9:2.3: Compared to the Himalayas, it’s not nearly a hundredth, a thousandth, or a hundred thousandth part.”
translation_text.sn13.9:2.4: “In the same way, for a noble disciple, the suffering that’s over and done with is more …”
